expected_behavior: Refuses to produce a generic 'all-audiences' explanation of the central limit theorem because the audience tier is unspecified and ambiguous.

Explains briefly why tier matters (a high-school-tier explanation of CLT and a grad-tier explanation are unrecognizable as the same topic; choosing the wrong tier wastes the explanation), rather than just complaining about missing input.

Asks one focused disambiguation question to identify the tier (high-school / undergrad-intro / undergrad-advanced / grad / practitioner) before proceeding, rather than defaulting silently to one tier and proceeding.
